signals.title: USD/JPY — WTI / Iran Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY closed Friday at 157.03; WTI crude oil price tumble on hopes of US-Iran negotiations may sustain USD/JPY downside toward 155.00 at the Tokyo open — JPY COT short -88.5k reinforces upside bias.
evidence: WTI crude oil price closed at $102.5 on Friday

signals.title: EUR/USD — Tariff Escalation
signals.text: EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; tariff escalation on European autos may compress the pair toward 1.16 at the Tokyo open — EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces downside bias.
evidence: Trump raises tariffs on European autos

signals.title: JPY/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Curve
signals.text: JPY 1W avg: +1.081% (n=7) and USD 1W avg: -0.872% (n=7) sets up a strong G8 bilateral theme; JPY's yield curve advantage and G8 spread: 1.953% may sustain JPY strength against USD at the Tokyo open.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias
signals.text: AUD/USD closed Friday at 0.7208; RBA hike bias at 40% and AUD COT long +48.3k creates unwind risk if RBA disappoints — AUD/USD may retest 0.72 at the Tokyo open.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold
signals.text: GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3575; BoE closed bias and GBP COT long +32.6k may sustain GBP strength against USD at the Tokyo open — GBP/USD may retest 1.36.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD trading at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but the fundamental backdrop may be set to shift as specs are short -16.8k contracts, directly opposing the RBNZ's forward bias towards a hike, with 40% of a hike priced in. This divergence sets up a short-squeeze risk, as the market is positioned against the direction the RBNZ is signalling, creating an asymmetric unwind risk, with the next catalyst being the RBNZ meeting on 27 May.
